objective: Architect a robust, dynamic backend that connects the user's Telegram Bot inputs to the Symphony frontend dashboard via Supabase.

active_thoughts: Deconstruct: The current website is static (hardcoded arrays in JS). The user wants their 'architect bot' (Athena/Telegram Bot) to take inputs and dynamically modify the website's data.

Lateral Prism - Tech View: We need a central source of truth. Supabase (PostgreSQL) is the perfect bridge. Telegram Bot writes to Supabase via POST/Python. Symphony JS fetches from Supabase via GET/REST.
Lateral Prism - Biz View: This reduces maintenance to zero. The user never has to touch `symphony-app.js` again to add a 'Want' or check off a task.
Lateral Prism - Risk View: Exposing Supabase anon keys in front-end JS requires Row Level Security (RLS) to prevent unauthorized writes, but for a local dashboard, anon read keys are acceptable.
Plan: Draft a robust 3-tier architecture plan (Telegram -> Supabase -> Frontend) in the implementation plan.

1.5_senior_principal_review.notes: Will perform a First Principles Breakdown of the Supabase schema to ensure all current hardcoded elements (Points, Wants/Needs, Food, Bloods) have a scalable table structure.
